mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS signals strengthening consumer spending which drives corporate revenues and earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of RSAFS monthly change vs. forward 20-day SPX returns is <= 0 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in UNRATE greater than 0.2 percentage points signals improving labor market conditions, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving equity prices higher over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.2 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward SPX return following UNRATE drops >0.2pp is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.10) over the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive monthly growth in INDPRO and HOUST indicates broad-based economic expansion with rising production and construction activity, robustly supporting corporate earnings and stock prices.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and HOUST grow month-over-month, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative more often than 50% of the time in the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment with CPI 12-month growth exceeding 3%, further increases in CPIAUCSL erode real purchasing power and compress equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods with CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%, the Rank IC of monthly CPIAUCSL change vs. forward 20-day SPX return is positive (contradicting the hypothesis) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects expanding aggregate demand, which directly improves corporate sales and profitability, lifting equity prices over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day SPX return is non-positive over any complete business cycle (peak-to-peak).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in PAYEMS exceeding 200,000 indicates strong job creation, boosting investor confidence in economic momentum and leading to immediate positive returns within the following trading week.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return after PAYEMS increases >200k is negative or zero over the test period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near zero and unemployment remains elevated, the combination of highly accommodative monetary policy and labor slack signals ongoing support for the economy, which is positive for equities over the medium term.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS <=0.5% and UNRATE >=7%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative in more than half of the occurrences in the out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary regime defined by UNRATE exceeding 6%, persistently high unemployment suppresses consumer demand and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: During recessionary periods (UNRATE >6%), the 60-day forward SPX return is positive more often than negative, contradicting the hypothesis.
